name: Chicken in Coconut Milk
recipeIngredient: 3 lb Chicken fryer, cut into portion-sized pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Towel dry the chicken.
2. In a large, heavy skillet, heat the coconut oil over medium-high heat.
3. Add the chicken pieces and fry until lightly browned on all sides. Remove from the skillet and set aside.
4. In the same skillet, sauté the onion for 3 minutes until softened.
5. Add the garlic and mushrooms to the skillet. Sauté for another 2-3 minutes until the vegetables are just tender.
6. Return the chicken pieces to the skillet with the onion mixture.
7. Stir in the coconut milk, salt, and pepper to taste.
8. Tie a bouquet garni using the parsley, hot pepper, and thyme leaves on one end of a strip of cheesecloth or kitchen twine; tie off the other end securely.
9. Add the bouquet garni to the skillet.
10. Cover and simmer over low heat for about 1¼ hours, stirring occasionally, until the chicken is cooked through and tender.
11. Remove the bouquet garni and the chicken with rice from the skillet.
Note: The author added 3 ripe plantains (sliced), a few Caribbean root vegetables (diced small) with a little brown sugar, 2 packets of Sazon spice mix, curry powder, and 2 tablespoons of rum to the sauce. The plantains melted into the sauce while the root vegetables held their shape, resulting in a sweet yet delicious dish over rice.
